'''Anchor Blue''' was a fierce [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] who participated in the free-for-all gladiatorial brawls in [[the Cauldron]] on [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]], during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]]. Anchor Blue fought in the Cauldron when [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] [[Mission to Rattatak|entered]] the fray and ultimately emerged victorious over the other gladiators.

==Biography==
This [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] lived<ref name="CE" /> during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> when he gained renown as a fierce combatant under the nickname<ref name="DB" /> Anchor Blue<ref name="Sticker">''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''</ref> in the [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] pits on the planet [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]]. Despite his natural strength, Houk's patron devised a way to further increase his ferocity through artificial means. Blue's patron secretly implanted a pain-inducing device in the base of the [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] attachment that had replaced his amputated hand. A remote control sent searing electrical charges through Houk's body, driving him into heightened rage. To subdue him afterward, the same device released powerful sedatives into his [[Blood/Legends|bloodstream]]. Anchor Blue developed a strong dependency on these illicit chemicals, which allowed his patron to exploit him even further.<ref name="DB" />

[[File:GladiatorsOnTheCauldron.jpg|280px|left|thumb|Anchor Blue and the other gladiators preparing to attack Asajj Ventress]]
Eventually, shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC" /> Anchor Blue ended up in [[the Cauldron]] arena.<ref name="CW" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on Rattatak, filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants who would win their wars.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, a fierce Houk, like many other gladiators,<ref name="CW" /> entered a deadly free-for-all battle,<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> but it was a rare gladiator indeed who could survive multiple fights there.<ref name="DBR" /> During one battle, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] attended the match, hoping to find a new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] among the fighters. Soon, the [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] entered the arena and, in a short time, defeated most of the remaining gladiators. The survivors united for a single attack against Ventress, but she used the [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to crush stone debris from the ceiling onto their heads. Anchor Blue was the first to be caught in the rockfall,<ref name="CW" /> which led to his death.<ref name="DB" />

==Personality==
[[File:AnchorBlueRoars.jpg|280px|right|thumb|Anchor Blue roars fiercely]]
A fierce [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] named Anchor Blue belonged to a species that had a well-deserved reputation for raw strength and short tempers. Their muscular bulk allowed them to dominate any tests of physical prowess. A [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattataki]] patron who owned Anchor Blue devised a way to further increase his ferocity through artificial means.<ref name="DB"  /> The Houk's right hand<ref  name="CW"  /> was replaced with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] that concealed a frenzy-triggering implant within the weapon's attachment. Blue's patron used a remote device to drive him into violent rages or to calm him with a chemical reservoir that pumped powerful sedatives into his [[Blood/Legends|bloodstream]].<ref name="CE"  /> Anchor Blue developed a strong dependency on these illicit chemicals, which allowed his patron to exploit him even further.<ref name="DB"  />

During the fight in the Cauldron, Anchor Blue fought naked, wearing only a tattered loincloth.<ref  name="CW"  />

==Behind the scenes==
Anchor Blue<ref name="Sticker" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The character was later given a name in ''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', a [[2004]] sticker book written by [[Simon Beecroft]],<ref name="DK">{{DK|url=us/9780756607593-ultimate-sticker-book-star-wars-clone-wars|text=Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars|nolive=1}}</ref> and short backstory in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CE" /> a [[2008]] reference book written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Bob Vitas]], and [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160596/the-complete-star-wars-encyclopedia-by-stephen-j-sansweet-pablo-hidalgo-bob-vitas-daniel-wallace/9780345477637|text=The Complete Star Wars® Encyclopedia}}</ref>
